skill.name: pitfalls-tanstack-query
skill.description: Guides reviews and implementations of TanStack Query v5 data fetching, mutations, query keys, cache invalidation, and optimistic updates.
skill.summary: Find and fix common TanStack Query v5 mistakes in React applications.
skill.author: BarisSozen

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review found no executable commands or cryptographic code. The alerts are false positives from markdown code fences, a TypeScript template literal, and frontmatter text.

low_findings.title: False Positive External Command Detections
low_findings.description: The static external command alerts are markdown fence markers and a TypeScript API URL template literal. No Ruby shell backtick execution or command execution path is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are visible markdown or TypeScript examples, not executable skill code. The only template literal is used as an API path example.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Detection
low_findings.description: The high-severity blocker alert points to frontmatter description text. No weak cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, or encryption code appears at this location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is metadata describing TanStack Query triggers. It contains no cryptographic operation or security-sensitive implementation.

content.value_statement: TanStack Query v5 bugs often cause stale data, duplicated requests, and broken loading states. This skill gives focused review steps and patterns for safer queries, mutations, and cache updates.
actual_capabilities: Checks whether query keys include stable URL paths and identifiers.
Reviews mutation success handlers for relevant query invalidation.
Highlights TanStack Query v5 loading state changes, including isPending.
Encourages typed responses with shared schema or domain types.
Shows optimistic update flow with cancel, snapshot, rollback, and settle steps.
limitations: Does not inspect a live application or runtime cache state by itself.
Does not replace official TanStack Query documentation for complete API details.
Does not generate a full data layer architecture from requirements alone.
Examples are focused on React and TanStack Query v5.

use_cases.title: Review Pull Requests
use_cases.description: Find stale cache risks, weak query keys, and outdated mutation loading checks before merge.

use_cases.title: Debug Stale Data
use_cases.description: Trace missing invalidation or mismatched keys when screens do not update after a mutation.

use_cases.title: Guide v5 Migration
use_cases.description: Check common migration details such as mutation state names and updated cache handling patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Check Basic Query Usage
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this TanStack Query v5 data fetching flow for query key quality, typing, and loading state issues.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single query.

prompt_templates.title: Find Mutation Cache Gaps
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these mutations and identify any missing invalidation, stale cache risks, or incorrect v5 mutation states.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of create, update, or delete flows.

prompt_templates.title: Debug Stale Screen Data
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze why this screen may show stale data after mutation. Focus on query key alignment and invalidation scope.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced debugging for inconsistent UI updates.

prompt_templates.title: Design Optimistic Updates
prompt_templates.prompt: Propose a TanStack Query v5 optimistic update strategy with snapshot, rollback, and final invalidation steps.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced design for responsive mutation flows.

output_examples.input: Review a query that uses a short key and fetches a strategy by identifier.
output: The query key is too broad for a detail request.
Use a stable key that includes the resource path and identifier.
Confirm the response type matches the expected strategy shape.

output_examples.input: Review a create mutation that navigates after success.
output: The mutation should invalidate the affected list query before or during navigation.
Without invalidation, the destination may show stale data.
Use the v5 mutation pending state for submit button feedback.

output_examples.input: Plan an optimistic update for editing an existing item.
output: Cancel related in-flight queries before writing optimistic data.
Store the previous cache value for rollback on error.
Invalidate the affected key after the mutation settles.

best_practices: Use stable, specific query keys that match the fetched resource.
Invalidate every query that can be affected by a successful mutation.
Use TanStack Query v5 state names when reviewing mutation loading behavior.
anti_patterns: Using broad query keys that cannot distinguish list and detail data.
Navigating after mutations without refreshing affected cached data.
Using outdated mutation loading state names from earlier versions.

faq.question: What version does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets TanStack Query v5 patterns and common migration pitfalls.

faq.question: Can it review existing React code?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the relevant query and mutation code for focused review.

faq.question: Does it run the application?
faq.answer: No. It provides code review guidance and reasoning from supplied source context.

faq.question: Does it cover optimistic updates?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers snapshot, rollback, cache write, and final invalidation steps.

faq.question: Can it help with stale data bugs?
faq.answer: Yes. It focuses on query key alignment and mutation invalidation problems.

faq.question: Is it only for Claude?
faq.answer: No. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
